element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary attack tool, fires in 8 directions (unique for NES platformers)
Depletes on enemy contact; full depletion or pit falls cause life loss
Aliens guard each sector; defeated by boomerang attacks
Upgrades to weapon spread (multiple boomerangs), range extension, and damage increase
Must locate agent in each sector to receive ID card for database access
Required to enter security room in each sector
Sector-specific boss fights; defeat required to obtain data tape
One tape per sector; all six required to unlock Control Center
Equips automatically; grants ability to shoot energy blasts (Power Blades) in 8 directions through most surfaces
Non-linear progression; player chooses any of 6 sectors in any order
Final stage; player must destroy alien overlord and restore Master Computer
Time running out causes life loss; pressure mechanic
Falling off screen results in instant death (unique to Power Blade vs sequels)
Allows restart at specific checkpoint; progression persistence

typical_relief_triggers: Obtaining data tape (sector disarm cutscene with relief music BGM0A)
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy spawn waves in confined spaces
Transitioning to Control Center (final area music BGM07 'Well!Well!Well!' described as foreboding)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear multi-stage structure with repeating tension-relief cycles per sector; six self-contained arc completions before climactic finale. Boss BGM (BGM0B) escalates tension; sector disarm cutscene music (BGM0A) provides relief. Time limit creates sustained low-level pressure throughout. Power-up collection breaks tension momentarily.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Boomerang attack in 8 directions suggests multi-directional exploration; agent location within sectors implies non-linear search patterns
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Not documented in sources; common NES convention of hidden items in ceiling/wall areas implied by power-up distribution but not explicitly described

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like introduction through Stage Select and Sector 1-2; basic boomerang mechanics and simple enemy patterns establish baseline
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Sectors 3-5 introduce escalating enemy density, more complex platforming hazards (pits, multi-level navigation), and more difficult boss patterns. Power-up collection becomes strategically important
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sector 6 and Control Center feature dense enemy placement, complex arena design, final boss with transformation (David Uncle becomes more dangerous alien form with BGM0C). Player must have mastered 8-direction aiming and boomerang upgrades
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (security room introductory shock)
Final Boss / Control Center (alien overlord with transformation mechanic)
Pit hazard intensity increases mid-game (instant death penalty unique to Power Blade)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 6 sectors follows: exploration → locate agent → receive ID card → navigate to security room → defeat boss → obtain data tape → sector disarm cutscene with BGM0A (relief music). Structurally identical repetition with thematic variation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Sector 1 (Rocket/Space Development BGM05) through Sector 6 (Residential Area BGM08)

notable_level_patterns.description: Security room access gated behind agent contact; forces non-linear search within sector before linear climax. Creates optional backtracking for power-ups before entering danger zone
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All six sectors employ this pattern

notable_level_patterns.description: Each sector has dedicated BGM reflecting its function: Sector 1 (Space Development), Sector 2 (Energy Development BGM06), Sector 3 (Life Research BGM03), Sector 4 (Weather Control BGM09), Sector 5 (Military Base BGM04), Sector 7 (Central Control Center BGM02). Music signals location/purpose
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Sector 3 (BIO theme) vs Sector 5 (BASE theme) convey different environmental contexts

notable_level_patterns.description: Control Center serves as climactic integration; requires all 6 tapes to access. Utilizes new boss theme (BGM0C) for alien overlord transformation. No sector select available; linear final sequence
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Control Center / Master Computer final battle

notable_level_patterns.description: Stage select screen allows any-order progression through 6 sectors (Mega Man-inspired). Map screen redesigned in US/EU version for clarity. Creates player agency and difficulty self-selection
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage Select (BGM01 Select BGM) gateway for all 6 sectors

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - comprehensive regional differences, music/SFX documentation, stage structure details)
wikipedia (gameplay mechanics, plot structure, boomerang/power-up systems, time limit/pit hazard rules)
